title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. bertolae
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. bertolae, also known as Bertolotti's bluebell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and expectorant, helping to relieve symptoms of urinary tract infections and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and aiding in the treatment of conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. In herbal preparations, Bertolotti's bluebell can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health ailments.
